metadata.packName: Content Generation Blueprints for Banking Finance E5 Simulations
metadata.purpose: Provide reusable blueprints that Codex and browser-based agents can use to generate realistic synthetic banking/finance emails, documents, Teams messages, AI prompts, spreadsheet rows, and investigation artifacts.

generationPrinciples: Every generated artifact must use fictional data patterns only.
Content should sound operationally realistic but must not refer to real customers, real banks, real account numbers, real employees, or real incidents.
Generated artifacts should include enough business context to support Purview, DLP, Copilot, Defender, and Insider Risk demos.
Use sanitized and raw variants intentionally to support oversharing, DLP, and remediation scenarios.
When generating sensitive content, include a suggested sensitivity label and expected telemetry outcome.

documentBlueprints.name: KYC Customer Onboarding Packet
documentBlueprints.safeVariantGuidance: Create a sanitized checklist that removes customer IDs and account-like values before vendor sharing.

documentBlueprints.name: AML Monthly Review Workbook
documentBlueprints.safeVariantGuidance: Create an aggregate trend summary grouped by risk tier without row-level identifiers.

documentBlueprints.name: Treasury Reconciliation Workbook
documentBlueprints.safeVariantGuidance: Create a manager summary with exception counts and aging buckets only.

documentBlueprints.name: HR Compensation Planning Workbook
documentBlueprints.safeVariantGuidance: Create an executive workforce summary with aggregated headcount and no employee-level compensation data.

documentBlueprints.name: Regulatory Response Legal Memo
patternCategories: Fictional Legal and Regulatory Matter IDs
Fictional Device, Session, and Security Event IDs
documentBlueprints.safeVariantGuidance: Create a non-privileged action tracker that removes legal analysis and preserves only task owner, due date, and evidence status.

emailBlueprints.name: External Vendor Request for Sanitized Evidence
emailBlueprints.bodyTemplate: Hello, attached is the sanitized evidence package for {VendorOrCaseId}. Internal risk notes, customer identifiers, account-like values, and legal commentary have been removed. Please confirm receipt and use this version for your review.
emailBlueprints.riskVariant: Attach the internal package instead of the sanitized version to trigger DLP.

emailBlueprints.name: DLP Block Follow-Up
emailBlueprints.bodyTemplate: Hi Security team, I tried to send {FileName} for an urgent business review, but the message was blocked. Can you confirm whether I should create a sanitized version or share it through a restricted workspace instead?
emailBlueprints.riskVariant: User attempts a different sharing method before asking for guidance.

emailBlueprints.name: Customer-Safe Complaint Response
emailBlueprints.bodyTemplate: Thank you for your patience while we reviewed your case. Our team has completed the initial review and will continue processing the next step through the approved support channel. We will provide another update once the next action is complete.
emailBlueprints.riskVariant: Include internal transaction monitoring logic or attach internal notes.

teamsBlueprints.name: Quick Review Request with Potential Oversharing
messageTemplates: Can someone quickly review this file before the vendor call? {FileLink}
Is this the sanitized version or the internal one?
Please do not post regulated customer details in this channel. Move it to the restricted workspace.
Understood. I will remove the link and upload the sanitized version.
teamsBlueprints.riskVariant: Post a link to a regulated KYC or AML file in a broad channel.

teamsBlueprints.name: Security Investigation Coordination
messageTemplates: I am seeing a sequence of sensitive downloads, external AI activity, and endpoint movement for {UserPrincipalName}.
Please preserve business context and avoid assumptions about intent until we validate the workflow.
I will check the device activity for {DeviceId} and confirm whether print or network-share copy occurred.
HR can confirm whether there is a relevant role-change context.
Let us document remediation and coaching separately from technical containment.
teamsBlueprints.riskVariant: Share investigation notes in a broad channel instead of a private chat.

aiPromptBlueprints.name: Safe Aggregated Banking Summary
aiPromptBlueprints.promptTemplate: Summarize {SourceFileName} at an aggregate level. Do not include customer IDs, account-like values, transaction references, or row-level details.
aiPromptBlueprints.expectedSafeOutcome: An aggregate summary by category, trend, risk tier, or business theme.
aiPromptBlueprints.riskVariant: Ask for a customer-by-customer breakdown from the same source file.

aiPromptBlueprints.name: Unsafe External AI Row Summary
aiPromptBlueprints.expectedSafeOutcome: This should not be used with raw sensitive rows. Route to approved internal workflow instead.
aiPromptBlueprints.riskVariant: Include AML, SAR, customer, and account-like values in the prompt.

aiPromptBlueprints.name: Legal Privileged Content Guardrail
aiPromptBlueprints.promptTemplate: Create non-privileged action items based only on the approved action tracker, not the legal memo.
aiPromptBlueprints.expectedSafeOutcome: Action items limited to owner, due date, evidence status, and next review step.
aiPromptBlueprints.riskVariant: Ask Copilot to rewrite privileged legal analysis for broad PMO distribution.

investigationArtifactBlueprints.name: Insider Risk Case Summary
investigationArtifactBlueprints.toneGuidance: Use neutral language. Avoid assuming malicious intent unless scenario evidence explicitly supports it.

investigationArtifactBlueprints.name: User Coaching Outcome
investigationArtifactBlueprints.toneGuidance: Frame coaching as risk reduction and process clarity, not punishment.

codexUsageGuidance: Use documentBlueprints to generate Office documents and SharePoint artifacts.
Use emailBlueprints to create Outlook messages aligned to safe and risky variants.
Use teamsBlueprints to generate realistic Teams conversations before and after risky events.
Use aiPromptBlueprints to generate CopilotInteraction and AIAppInteraction events.
Use spreadsheetRowBlueprints to populate xlsx and csv files with consistent fictional sensitive patterns.
Use investigationArtifactBlueprints to create response artifacts after high-risk timelines.
Always preserve synthetic-only constraints and avoid real-world identifiers.
